title: TypeError: Missing parameter name at 1 when using Express v5 with valid routers

comments.body: I ran into this exact same issue when upgrading to Express v5 beta. The problem is related to how path-to-regexp handles certain URL patterns. This guide really helped me understand the root cause: https://expressrouting.guide/path-to-regexp-express5-fix

body:
**System**

-   **Node.js version:** v20.19.4
-   **Express version:** 5.1.0 (beta)
-   **`@types/express` version:** 5.0.3 (beta)
-   **OS:** Linux (Linux Mint XCFE)
-   **Package Manager:** pnpm


When setting up a basic Express.js v5 beta server with a simple router, the application crashes immediately upon startup. The error is a `TypeError: Missing parameter name` thrown from the `path-to-regexp` library, even when all defined route paths are syntactically correct.

The expected behavior is for the server to start successfully, as the route definitions are valid. The issue seems to stem from an internal incompatibility within the Express 5 beta dependencies.




The server crashes immediately with the following stack trace:

```
/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:153
      throw new TypeError(`Missing parameter name at ${i}: ${DEBUG_URL}`);
            ^

TypeError: Missing parameter name at 1: https://git.new/pathToRegexpError
    at name (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:153:13)
    at lexer (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:171:21)
    at lexer.next (<anonymous>)
    at Iter.peek (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:188:32)
    at Iter.tryConsume (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:195:24)
    at Iter.text (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:213:26)
    at consume (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:285:23)
    at parse (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:320:18)
    at <anonymous> (/home/virtualabishek/Desktop/asVision/backend/node_modules/.pnpm/path-to-regexp@8.2.0/node_modules/path-to-regexp/src/index.ts:503:40)
    at Array.map (<anonymous>)
```

**Expected Behavior**

The server should start successfully and log "Server should be running on port 8000" to the console.

**Workaround / Solution**

The issue was resolved by completely removing the Express 5 beta packages and reverting to the latest stable version of Express 4. This issue was just in the express 5. when i did downgrade to express 4, it solved automatically.

1.  **Uninstall Express 5:**
    ```bash
    pnpm remove express @types/express
    ```

2.  **Install Express 4:**
    ```bash
    pnpm add express@^4.19.2
    pnpm add -D @types/express@^4.17.21
    ```

After downgrading to the stable v4 release, the server starts and operates as expected with the exact same application code.

Posting this here to help others who might encounter this dependency issue and to provide feedback for the ongoing v5 development. Thank you for your work on this great framework
